deploymentType: Phased Infrastructure Deployment with Health Validation

metadata.name: Ectropy Enterprise Infrastructure Deployment
metadata.purpose: Deploy core infrastructure (PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO) and Speckle BIM stack with comprehensive health validation and phased rollout approach
metadata.author: Claude (Enterprise Integration)

executiveSummary.description: Automated deployment script for Ectropy enterprise infrastructure including core databases (PostgreSQL, Redis), Speckle BIM platform (GraphQL API, Web UI, S3 storage), and application services (API Gateway, MCP Server, Web Dashboard). Implements phased deployment with health validation at each stage.
keyChanges: Implements phased deployment: Core → Speckle → Application
Health checks with configurable timeouts and retry logic
Speckle database auto-creation and migration support
MinIO S3 storage for BIM file management
prerequisites: Docker Desktop or Docker Engine running

systems: Speckle BIM platform (server, frontend, preview, webhook, fileimport)
impact.downtime: Zero downtime for fresh deployments; potential brief interruption if updating existing services
impact.rollbackTime: 5 minutes (automated container stop and previous configuration restore)

preFlightChecks.name: Run Pre-Flight Check Script
preFlightChecks.description: Execute automated pre-flight validation script (can be skipped with -SkipPreFlight flag)

onFailure.message: Pre-flight checks failed. Resolve issues or use -SkipPreFlight to override (not recommended)
Docker is not running. Start Docker Desktop or Docker Engine service.

preFlightChecks.name: Docker Compose File Validation
preFlightChecks.description: Ensure docker-compose.development.yml exists and is valid

onFailure.message: Docker Compose file is missing or invalid

preFlightChecks.name: Environment Variables Configured
preFlightChecks.expectedResult: At least one environment file exists

onFailure.message: No environment file found. Services may use default values.

preFlightChecks.name: Critical Ports Available
preFlightChecks.description: Verify ports 3000, 4000, 5432, 6379, 8080, 9000 are not in use

onFailure.message: Required ports are in use. Stop conflicting services.

preFlightChecks.name: System Resources Available
preFlightChecks.description: Check available RAM and disk space

onFailure.message: Low memory detected. Close unnecessary applications.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure Deployment
phases.description: Deploy PostgreSQL database, Redis cache, and initialize Speckle database

steps.name: Start PostgreSQL Database
steps.description: Launch PostgreSQL container with persistent volume
steps.purpose: Initialize primary database for Ectropy and Speckle data

onFailure.message: Failed to start PostgreSQL. Check Docker logs.

steps.name: Wait for PostgreSQL Initialization
steps.description: Poll pg_isready until database accepts connections (max 60 seconds)
steps.command: for ($i = 1; $i -le 12; $i++) { docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 pg_isready -U postgres; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { break }; Start-Sleep 5 }
steps.purpose: Ensure database is fully initialized before dependent services start

onFailure.message: PostgreSQL failed to initialize. Check logs: docker logs ectropy-postgres-1

steps.name: Start Redis Cache
steps.description: Launch Redis container for session and cache management
steps.purpose: Initialize distributed cache for API Gateway and application state

steps.name: Verify Redis Health
steps.description: Test Redis connectivity with PING command
steps.command: Start-Sleep 5; docker exec ectropy-redis-1 redis-cli ping
steps.purpose: Confirm Redis is accepting connections

steps.name: Create Speckle Database
steps.description: Create dedicated Speckle database for BIM platform data
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c 'CREATE DATABASE speckle'
steps.purpose: Initialize separate database for Speckle BIM metadata and stream data

steps.name: Check Migration Status
steps.description: Verify if Speckle tables exist in database
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c '\dt speckle*'
steps.purpose: Determine if migrations need to be applied
steps.expectedOutput: List of speckle tables or empty result

phases.name: Speckle BIM Platform Deployment
phases.description: Deploy MinIO S3 storage, Speckle Server, Frontend, and support services

steps.name: Start MinIO S3 Storage
steps.description: Launch MinIO for BIM file storage
steps.purpose: Provide S3-compatible object storage for Speckle file uploads and previews

steps.name: Wait for MinIO Health
steps.description: Poll MinIO health endpoint until ready (60 second timeout)
steps.purpose: Ensure object storage is ready before Speckle server starts

onFailure.message: MinIO did not become healthy. Check logs: docker logs ectropy-minio-1

steps.name: Start Speckle Server (GraphQL API)
steps.description: Launch Speckle Server with database and storage connections
steps.purpose: Deploy core Speckle GraphQL API for BIM data management

steps.name: Wait for Speckle Server Initialization
steps.description: Poll Speckle readiness endpoint for up to 60 seconds
steps.command: Start-Sleep 30; for ($i = 1; $i -le 12; $i++) { docker exec ectropy-speckle-server-1 wget -q -O - http://localhost:3000/readiness; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { break }; Start-Sleep 5 }
steps.purpose: Ensure Speckle server has completed database migrations and is ready

onFailure.message: Speckle server may still be initializing. Check logs: docker logs ectropy-speckle-server-1

steps.name: Start Speckle Frontend (Web UI)
steps.description: Launch Speckle web interface
steps.purpose: Deploy Speckle user interface for BIM model viewing and management

steps.name: Start Speckle Support Services
steps.description: Launch preview service, webhook handler, and file import processor
steps.command: docker-compose -f docker-compose.development.yml up -d speckle-preview speckle-webhook speckle-fileimport
steps.purpose: Enable BIM preview generation, webhook notifications, and file import capabilities

phases.name: Ectropy Application Services Deployment
phases.description: Deploy API Gateway, MCP Server, and Web Dashboard

steps.name: Start API Gateway
steps.description: Launch Ectropy REST API Gateway with Speckle integration
steps.purpose: Deploy main API layer connecting frontend to database and Speckle BIM platform

steps.name: Verify API Gateway Health
steps.description: Poll /health endpoint until API is ready
steps.purpose: Confirm API Gateway is accepting requests and connected to dependencies

onFailure.message: API Gateway may still be starting. Check logs: docker logs ectropy-api-gateway-1

steps.name: Start MCP Server
steps.description: Launch Model Context Protocol server for AI integration
steps.purpose: Deploy MCP server for Claude AI integration and runbook management

steps.name: Verify MCP Server Health
steps.description: Poll MCP health endpoint
steps.purpose: Confirm MCP server is operational

onFailure.message: MCP Server may still be starting

steps.name: Start Web Dashboard
steps.description: Launch React frontend application
steps.purpose: Deploy user-facing web application with BIM viewer integration

steps.name: Verify Web Dashboard Health
steps.description: Wait for web server to respond
steps.purpose: Confirm frontend is accessible and serving content

onFailure.message: Web Dashboard may still be building

phases.name: Post-Deployment Validation
phases.description: Verify all services are running and provide next steps

steps.name: Display Container Status
steps.description: Show status of all Ectropy containers
steps.purpose: Provide visual confirmation of all running services
steps.expectedOutput: Table showing all containers in Up state

steps.name: Display Next Steps
steps.description: Show post-deployment instructions for database migration and Speckle setup
steps.command: Write-Host instructions for migration, Speckle user creation, environment updates, smoke tests, and access URLs
steps.purpose: Guide operator through required manual configuration steps
steps.expectedOutput: Formatted instructions for: migration script, Speckle admin setup, token configuration, smoke tests, application URLs

performanceThresholds.measurement: Time from 'docker compose up' to healthy state

triggerConditions: Phase 1 core infrastructure fails to start
Phase 2 Speckle services fail to initialize
Phase 3 application services cannot connect to dependencies
Critical health checks fail after deployment

steps.name: Stop All Running Containers
steps.purpose: Gracefully stop all services to prevent data corruption

steps.name: Preserve Database Volumes
steps.purpose: Verify data volumes are intact for potential recovery

steps.name: Restore Previous Configuration
steps.purpose: Revert to last known good compose and environment configuration

steps.name: Restart with Previous Configuration
steps.purpose: Attempt to restore service with previous configuration

steps.name: Verify Rollback Success
steps.purpose: Confirm services are running after rollback

verification.successCriteria: All critical services (postgres, redis, api-gateway) are running and healthy

keyMetrics.alertOn: Any container enters unhealthy or restarting state

successCriteria.criterion: All Phase 1 infrastructure services (postgres, redis) start and pass health checks
successCriteria.validation: pg_isready and redis-cli ping both succeed
psql can connect to speckle database

successCriteria.criterion: Phase 2 Speckle BIM platform services running (minio, speckle-server, speckle-frontend)
Phase 3 Ectropy application services operational (api-gateway, mcp-server, web-dashboard)
successCriteria.validation: All services respond to health checks within 60 seconds

successCriteria.criterion: No error messages in container logs
successCriteria.validation: docker compose logs shows no FATAL or ERROR messages
HTTP 200 response from Speckle web UI

required.name: DATABASE_URL
required.description: PostgreSQL connection string for Ectropy database

required.name: SPECKLE_SERVER_TOKEN
required.description: Speckle Personal Access Token for API authentication

supportedQueries.query: How do I deploy the Ectropy infrastructure?
supportedQueries.returns: Full deployment runbook with phased approach

supportedQueries.query: What services are deployed in Phase 1?
How long does Speckle deployment take?

supportedQueries.returns: 8 service health checks including database, cache, BIM platform, and application layers

supportedQueries.query: How do I roll back a failed deployment?
supportedQueries.returns: 5-step rollback procedure with container stop and configuration restore

supportedQueries.query: What are the post-deployment next steps?
supportedQueries.returns: Phase 4: Database migration, Speckle admin user creation, environment token update, smoke tests

decisionSupport: Use -SkipPreFlight only if you've manually verified all prerequisites
Use -CoreOnly to deploy just databases without Speckle or applications
Use -SpeckleOnly to deploy only Speckle stack (requires core infrastructure already running)
Use -Verbose for detailed logging during deployment
Monitor health check timeouts - increase if services need more startup time
Always run smoke tests after deployment before declaring success

architectureAlignment.routingConfig: Deploys services matching routing-architecture.json port allocations
architectureAlignment.oauthConfig: Speckle OAuth callbacks must be configured in .env.local after token creation

knownIssues.title: Speckle Server Slow Initialization
knownIssues.impact: First-time Speckle deployment can take 90+ seconds
knownIssues.mitigation: Health check includes 60 second timeout with retry logic
knownIssues.action: Monitor logs for migration progress: docker logs ectropy-speckle-server-1

knownIssues.title: Speckle Token Must Be Manually Created
knownIssues.impact: API Gateway cannot connect to Speckle until token is configured
knownIssues.mitigation: Post-deployment step guides token creation and environment update
knownIssues.action: Follow Phase 4 instructions to create Speckle admin user and generate token

knownIssues.title: High Memory Usage During Initial Build
knownIssues.impact: Docker may consume 6-8GB RAM during image builds and startup
knownIssues.mitigation: Pre-flight check validates available system resources
knownIssues.action: Close unnecessary applications or increase Docker Desktop memory limit

knownIssues.title: Port 8080 Conflict with Speckle Frontend
knownIssues.impact: Deployment fails if port 8080 is already in use
knownIssues.action: Stop conflicting service or modify docker-compose.development.yml port mapping
